results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THIS PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP A CENTER TO CHARACTERIZE THE ENVIRONMENTAL STRESSORS THAT AFFECT CHILDREN'S SCHOOL ABSENTEEISM AND EVALUATE NEW INTERVENTION METHODS.ACTIVITIES:THE OBJECTIVE OF THIS RESEARCH IS TO ASSESS CHILDREN'S HEALTH IMPACTS OF EARLY LIFE STAGE STRESSOR EXPOSURE AND TO INTRODUCE AFFORDABLE INTERVENTIONAL METHODS TO REDUCE HEALTH EFFECTS RELATING TO ENVIRONMENTAL CHEMICAL AND NON CHEMICAL STRESSORS. 1) THE ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS WHICH RELATE TO HEALTH ISSUES AND CAUSE SCHOOL ABSENTEEISM WILL BE IDENTIFIED AND CATEGORIZED. 2) INTERVENTION TECHNOLOGIES WILL ALSO BE COMPARED FOR EFFICIENCY IN MITIGATING CHILD HEALTH IMPACTS. 3) THIS PROJECT WILL ALSO ESTABLISH A DATA DRIVEN CHILDREN'S HEALTH AND SOCIAL VULNERABILITY INDEX TO GUIDE FUTURE DECISION MAKING AND ASSESS THE HEALTH DISPARITIES AND ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE OF RURAL SCHOOLS. SUBRECIPIENT:THE TOTAL COST FOR SUBAWARDS IS $1,223,932, WITH $552,166 TO UNIVERSITY OF NORTH TEXAS, $597,341 TO UNIVERSITY OF OKLAHOMA - TULSA AND $74,425 TO UNIVERSITY OF OKLAHOMA - NORMAN. THE UNIVERSITY OF NORTH TEXAS WILL LEAD RESEARCH TO CHARACTERIZE AND INTERVENE CHEMICAL AND NON-CHEMICAL STRESSORS TO REDUCE SCHOOL ABSENTEEISM CAUSED BY GASTROINTESTINAL AND RESPIRATORY DISEASES USING MIXED METHODS. THE UNIVERSITY OF OKLAHOMA -TULSA WILL BE LEADING THE TRANSLATION CORE. OU NORMAN WILL DEVELOP  MICROCLIMATIC DATA COLLECTION AND DETERMINE SOCIOECONOMIC FACTORSOUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS DOCUMENTING THE CHARACTERIZATION AND EVALUATION OF THE CHEMICAL AND NON CHEMICAL STRESSORS THAT AFFECT SCHOOL ABSENTEEISM AND THE EFFECTIVENESS OF INDIVIDUAL AND COMBINED INTERVENTION METHODS. DATA WILL ALSO BE COLLECTED TO PRODUCE A PUBLIC CHILD HEALTH VULNERABILITY INDEX TO PREDICT THE SCHOOL ABSENTEEISM CAUSED BY GASTROINTESTINAL AND RESPIRATORY DISEASES. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES INCLUDE RURAL AGRICULTURAL COMMUNITIES, STATE AND LOCAL GOVERNMENTS, ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
